top_signals.title: Sinews of War at Sea: The Armed Services Need a Common Watercraft Family
top_signals.summary:
To sustain future maritime operations, the U.S military will need to run supplies through an environment that spans thousands of miles of open ocean, denied ports, contested straits, and archipelagic chokepoints against adversaries that have spent decades studying how to target American logistics. That problem does not require one identical vessel for every mission. It does require a more common family of watercraft for the manned ships that carry cargo and vehicles inside a theater, built for scale, interoperability, and wartime replacement. Getting this right is arguably the most important acquisition problem the joint force faces in the next decade.So

The post Sinews of War at Sea: The Armed Services Need a Common Watercraft Family appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises on export orders
top_signals.summary: Investors increasingly view China's two-speed growth with robust export and tepid domestic demand as a defining long-term feature of the Chinese economy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: BOK’s Shin Sees Need for Rate Hike Ahead of Next Week’s Meeting
top_signals.summary: Bank of Korea Governor Shin Hyun Song told parliament that policy rates will need to rise at an appropriate time, reinforcing the bank’s hawkish stance just a week before a widely expected rate hike.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
